front: Você gostaria de jantar comigo hoje à noite?
pronunciation: voh-SEH goos-tah-REE-ah jee zhahn-TAHR koong-MEE-goo OH-zhee ah NOY-chee?
back: Would you like to have dinner with me tonight?
context: A standard, polite invitation using the conditional 'gostaria' (would like).

front: Que tal irmos ao cinema no próximo sábado?
pronunciation: kee TAW EER-moos ow see-NEH-mah noo PROH-see-moo SAH-bah-doo?
back: How about we go to the movies next Saturday?
context: Using the classic 'Que tal...' structure to propose a casual group activity.

front: Estou livre na terça-feira, podemos marcar algo.
pronunciation: ees-TOY LEE-vree nah TEHR-sah FAY-rah, poo-DEH-moos mahr-KAHR AWL-goo.
back: I'm free on Tuesday, we can schedule something.
context: Using 'livre' (free) to indicate availability and 'marcar algo' (schedule something) as a plan pivot.

front: Adoraria, mas já tenho outros planos.
pronunciation: ah-doo-rah-REE-ah, mahs zhah TEYNG-yoo OH-troos PLAH-noos.
back: I would love to, but I already have other plans.
context: A polite, graceful way to decline an invitation.

front: O que você acha de irmos à praia?
pronunciation: oo kee voh-SEH AH-shah jee EER-moos ah PRY-ah?
back: What do you think about us going to the beach?
context: Using 'O que você acha de...' to solicit an opinion on a potential plan.

front: Sim, adoraria participar! Onde nos encontramos?
pronunciation: seeyng, ah-doo-rah-REE-ah pahr-chee-see-PAHR! OHN-jee noos eeyn-kohn-TRAH-moos?
back: Yes, I'd love to participate! Where shall we meet?
context: Enthusiastically accepting an invitation and arranging a meeting point using the reflexive 'nos encontramos'.

front: Infelizmente, não poderei ir nesta ocasião.
pronunciation: een-feh-lee-ZEHN-chee, nohng poo-deh-RAY EER NEHS-tah oh-kah-zy-OHNG.
back: Unfortunately, I will not be able to go on this occasion.
context: A formal, professional way to decline plans.

front: Podemos deixar para outro dia?
pronunciation: poo-DEH-moos day-ZHAHR PAH-rah OH-troo JEE-ah?
back: Can we leave it for another day?
context: Proposing a reschedule for plans that cannot happen currently.

front: Estou muito ocupado esta semana.
pronunciation: ees-TOY MOOI-too oh-koo-PAH-doo EHS-tah seh-MAH-nah.
back: I am very busy this week.
context: Stating unavailability due to a high workload.

front: Vamos tomar um café na próxima semana?
pronunciation: VAH-moos toh-MAHR oong kah-FEH nah PROH-see-mah seh-MAH-nah?
back: Shall we grab a coffee next week?
context: A classic 'get to know you' or 'catch up' social request.

front: Claro, que horas seria melhor para você?
pronunciation: KLAH-roo, kee OH-rahs seh-REE-ah meh-LYOHR PAH-rah voh-SEH?
back: Sure, what time would be better for you?
context: Accepting an invitation and deferring the scheduling decision to the other party.

front: Desculpe, eu esqueci completamente do nosso compromisso.
pronunciation: dehs-KOOL-pee, ew ehs-keh-SEE kohn-pleh-tah-MEHN-chee doo NOH-soo kohm-proo-MEE-soo.
back: Sorry, I completely forgot about our appointment/commitment.
context: Apologizing for a social mistake using 'compromisso' (appointment/obligation).

front: Podemos confirmar nossa ida amanhã?
pronunciation: poo-DEH-moos kohn-feer-MAHR NOH-sah EE-dah ah-mah-NYAH?
back: Can we confirm our going tomorrow?
context: Following up to finalize plans using 'ida' (act of going).

front: A que horas começa a festa?
pronunciation: ah kee OH-rahs koh-MEH-sah ah FEHS-tah?
back: What time does the party start?
context: Querying structural event timing.

front: Preciso confirmar com o Deni primeiro.
pronunciation: pree-SEE-zoo kohn-feer-MAHR koong oo Deni pree-MAY-roo.
back: I need to confirm with Deni first.
context: Using the 'pending spouse check' strategy before committing to social plans.

front: Que bom que você conseguiu vir!
pronunciation: kee boong kee voh-SEH kohn-seh-GEEW VEER!
back: How good that you managed to come!
context: A welcoming phrase acknowledging that plans were successfully met.

front: Vamos sair para jantar em algum lugar diferente?
pronunciation: VAH-moos sah-EER PAH-rah zhahn-TAHR ahyng aw-GOONG loo-GAHR jee-feh-REHN-chee?
back: Shall we go out for dinner somewhere different?
context: Suggesting an activity change to break a routine.

front: Não tenho certeza se posso, tenho um curso.
pronunciation: nohng TEYNG-yoo sehr-TEH-zah see POH-soo, TEYNG-yoo oong KOOR-soo.
back: I'm not sure if I can, I have a course.
context: Softly declining due to educational commitments.

front: Você está livre na sexta-feira à noite?
pronunciation: voh-SEH ees-TAH LEE-vree nah SEHKS-tah FAY-rah ah NOY-chee?
back: Are you free on Friday night?
context: Directly querying availability for social time.

front: Quem mais vai estar lá?
pronunciation: kayng mays vy ees-TAHR lah?
back: Who else will be there?
context: Querying the social circle/list of attendees.

front: Sinto muito, não vai ser possível.
pronunciation: SEEN-too MOOI-too, nohng vy sayr poo-SEE-vew.
back: I'm very sorry, it won't be possible.
context: A final, firm way to decline plans.

front: Espero que você possa vir na próxima.
pronunciation: ehs-PEH-roo kee voh-SEH POH-sah VEER nah PROH-see-mah.
back: I hope you can come next time.
context: A polite inclusionary phrase to encourage future social connections.

front: Você prefere almoçar ou jantar?
pronunciation: voh-SEH preh-FEH-ree ahl-moo-SAHR ow zhahn-TAHR?
back: Do you prefer to have lunch or dinner?
context: Giving the other person a binary choice to facilitate planning.

front: Podemos marcar às sete da noite?
pronunciation: poo-DEH-moos mahr-KAHR ahs SEH-chee dah NOY-chee?
back: Can we schedule it for seven at night?
context: Setting a precise temporal marker for plans.

front: O evento parece muito interessante.
pronunciation: oo eh-VEHN-too pah-REH-see MOOI-too eeyn-teh-reh-SAHN-chee.
back: The event seems very interesting.
context: Positive reinforcement of an invitation to encourage the plans.

front: Avisarei se algo mudar nos meus planos.
pronunciation: ah-vee-zah-RAY see AWL-goo moo-DAHR noos MEW-ohs PLAH-noos.
back: I will let you know if something changes in my plans.
context: Indicating potential flexibility for future scheduling shifts.

front: Não precisa trazer nada, eu cuido de tudo.
pronunciation: nohng pree-SEE-zah trah-ZEHR NAH-dah, ew KOO-ee-doo jee TOO-doo.
back: You don't need to bring anything, I'll take care of everything.
context: Host etiquette reassuring an invited guest regarding contribution requirements.

front: Vamos combinar os detalhes mais tarde?
pronunciation: VAH-moos kohn-bee-NAHR ooz deh-TAH-lyes mays TAHR-jee?
back: Shall we decide the details later?
context: Postponing logistics to a later point once the plan is broadly accepted.

front: Foi um prazer conversar com você, até breve!
pronunciation: foy oong prah-ZEHR kohn-vehr-SAHR koong voh-SEH, ah-TEH BREH-vee!
back: It was a pleasure talking to you, see you soon!
context: A warm social sign-off used after making or wrapping up plans.

front: Estou ansioso para esse encontro.
pronunciation: ees-TOY ahn-syo-zoo PAH-rah EHS-ee eeyn-kohn-troo.
back: I'm looking forward to this meeting/encounter.
context: Expressing positive anticipation for planned events. Female speakers use 'ansiosa'.

front: Você quer carona para o evento?
pronunciation: voh-SEH kehr kah-ROH-nah PAH-rah oo eh-VEHN-too?
back: Do you want a ride to the event?
context: A helpful social offer using the informal noun 'carona' (lift/ride).

front: Dá para encontrar um lugar mais silencioso?
pronunciation: DAH PAH-rah eeyn-kohn-TRAHR oong loo-GAHR mays see-lehn-syo-zoo?
back: Is it possible to find a quieter place?
context: Suggesting a venue shift for better conversation conditions.

front: Ainda não tenho certeza se estarei disponível.
pronunciation: ah-EEYN-dah nohng TEYNG-yoo sehr-TEH-zah see ees-tah-RAY jee-see-PONE-ee-vew.
back: I still don't know for sure if I'll be available.
context: Keeping options open without committing to a firm response.

front: Vamos marcar de novo qualquer dia desses?
pronunciation: VAH-moos mahr-KAHR jee NOH-voo kwaw-KEHR JEE-ah DEH-sehs?
back: Shall we schedule it again some day?
context: Closing a meeting by proactively setting the stage for future social interaction.

front: Que ótimo encontrar você aqui!
pronunciation: kee OH-chee-moo eeyn-kohn-TRAHR voh-SEH ah-KEE!
back: How great to meet/find you here!
context: An unplanned, enthusiastic social interaction starter when encountering someone unexpectedly.
